description: A practiced hand turns wood, clay, fibre, or another medium into a deliberate work. 'theko' is making carried out with learned skill. It does not certify that the result is safe or successful; 'pokera' (competent) reports ability against a stated criterion, and 'thonuki' (fail) reports a missed requirement.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let air pass along it into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Press the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k', release it, and round the lips for middle-height back 'o'.
sound_symbolism: Controlled dental breath meets the firmer closure of 'k', then rounds out. The movement has the measured finish of a practised stroke.
usage_notes: The crafted work is the direct object, and its material or tool takes instrumental 'roe'. In noun position, 'theko' names craft as an activity or one piece of skilled work.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Craft lets construction, surface, and ornament remain legible in the work instead of disguising how the thing was made.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Much craft knowledge passes by watching a practiced hand, attempting the movement, and being corrected at the work itself.

semantic_domains.activity: making carried out through learned practical skill
semantic_domains.creation: producing a deliberate work in a chosen material or medium
semantic_domains.wisdom: knowledge held and demonstrated through repeated practice
